howItWorks.intro: This automation payback calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Monthly Savings = (Hours Saved × Labor Rate) + Error Cost Reduction
Net Savings = Monthly Savings - Maintenance Cost
Payback Period = Implementation Cost ÷ Net Monthly Savings
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is a good payback period for automation?
faq.answer: Most companies target 12-18 months for automation payback. Under 6 months is excellent. Over 24 months carries more risk from changing requirements. Simple RPA bots often pay back in 3-6 months.

faq.question: What costs should I include in automation implementation?
faq.answer: Include: software licenses, development/configuration time, integration costs, testing, training, change management, and a contingency buffer of 15-20% for unexpected issues.

faq.question: How do I calculate the fully-loaded labor rate?
faq.answer: Fully-loaded rate includes salary, benefits (typically 25-40% of salary), office space, equipment, and management overhead. A $60K salary usually has a fully-loaded rate of $40-$50/hour.
